label: Supervised learning

node.label: Supervised learning
node.definition: A learner maps inputs to labeled targets using an explicit error signal.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Supervised learning is modeled as depending on Datasets, sampling & splits, Labels, targets, rewards & preferences and feeding downstream nodes such as Semi-supervised & weak supervision, Preference learning, Active learning & curriculum learning.
examples: atlas downstream link to Semi-supervised & weak supervision
atlas downstream link to Preference learning
atlas downstream link to Active learning & curriculum learning
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Datasets, sampling & splits, Labels, targets, rewards & preferences.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Supervised learning has a `uses` dependency on Datasets, sampling & splits because Datasets, sampling & splits is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Supervised learning has a `uses` dependency on Labels, targets, rewards & preferences because Labels, targets, rewards & preferences is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Supervised learning has a `uses` dependency on Optimization theory because Optimization theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
